mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously during easing cycles, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through lower cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% on 2009-03-01 indicates extreme easing
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment declines exceeding 0.5% trigger recession signals that cause equity investors to reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133,019k on 2009-03-01 reflects ongoing job losses
regime_hint: post-shock recession with accelerating labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when PAYEMS declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by > 5% month-over-month, forward earnings expectations deteriorate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side recession risk that single-series signals miss.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 583k on 2009-02-01 reflects housing collapse
regime_hint: demand-side recession with synchronized consumer and housing weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 98 (2017=100) signal supply-side contraction and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This triggers negative equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices near-term earnings downside from production shutdowns and inventory adjustments.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.3755 on 2009-03-01 reflects industrial collapse
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return when INDPRO < 98 is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate declines below 0% (recession) combined with unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8% signals dual demand and labor market deterioration. This interaction triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice recession probability and earnings risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.5% on 2009-03-01 reflects recession labor market
regime_hint: recession regime with synchronized GDP contraction and unemployment rise
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when both conditions triggered, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining month-over-month signals deflationary pressure that increases real debt burdens and reduces consumer purchasing power. This deflation signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate margins compress and consumer demand weakens.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.714 on 2009-03-01 reflects deflationary environment
regime_hint: deflationary recession with falling price levels
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return during deflationary months is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.5% and remains elevated for 3+ consecutive months, signaling persistent labor market weakness that constrains consumer spending and corporate hiring. This sustained high unemployment produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.5% on 2009-03-01 with rising trend
regime_hint: post-recession labor market with persistent unemployment elevation
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during sustained high unemployment periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 600k units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth below 1% month-over-month signals weakening consumer and housing demand. This dual demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices near-term economic growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 583k on 2009-02-01 reflects housing crisis
regime_hint: demand-side shock with synchronized housing and retail weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression model.
